nodes.label: AI as force multiplier for efficiency
nodes.label_negative: AI as insufficient or requiring rework
nodes.grounding_span: using it as a force multiplier to boost efficiency

nodes.label: AI as coding assistant
nodes.grounding_span: I don't know how to code well...this has added value because I know the kinds of tools I want to create and use

nodes.label: AI use for personal communication
nodes.label_negative: AI failing to match tone and phrasing
nodes.grounding_span: Copilot doesn't seem to get things right well enough for me, in terms of better understanding my tone and phrasing patterns

nodes.label: AI as collaborative partner
nodes.label_negative: AI as independent agent with mixed results
nodes.grounding_span: I tend to be more collaborative, but I have tested out ChatGPT's Agent capability...results have been mixed

nodes.label: Belief in AI capability improvement over time
nodes.grounding_span: the release of a new model to crack the problem...when OpenAI released o1, and it was able to solve a coding issue that 4o hadn't been able to solve

nodes.label: AI for high-attention and complex tasks
nodes.grounding_span: AI could alleviate the burdens of addressing the more common issues...in order for my teammates and me to focus on more advanced things

nodes.label: Technical capability and problem-solving
nodes.grounding_span: I know the kinds of tools I want to create and use

nodes.label: Authentic personal expression
nodes.grounding_span: better understanding my tone and phrasing patterns

nodes.label: Continuous improvement and innovation
nodes.grounding_span: the release of a new model to crack the problem

nodes.label: Professional growth and skill maintenance
nodes.grounding_span: focus on more advanced things that the AI can't handle

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI as a helpful tool
nodes.grounding_span: It was amazing...the output was easily over 100 lines of code

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for email drafting
nodes.grounding_span: Copilot doesn't seem to get things right well enough for me

nodes.label: Impressed but cautious about AI agent capability
nodes.grounding_span: Some were good, others not so much, but I came away impressed

nodes.label: Frustration with iterative coding failures
nodes.grounding_span: I would get frustrated when I would work on developing a script, and despite many turns and iterations, it wouldn't work

nodes.label: Optimism about future AI integration
nodes.grounding_span: my dream is to have a chatbot integrated into all of the project chats

nodes.label: Iterative refinement — upfront specification then back-and-forth adjustment
nodes.grounding_span: I try to be as specific and comprehensive as possible in the beginning...but there's always bound to be some iteration

nodes.label: Model-switching strategy to overcome limitations
nodes.grounding_span: I'd hop between different models and sometimes one model would find a solution where the previous one was unable to
